description: The first glimpse over a ridge can draw 'wao' before any description is ready. It expresses surprise or astonishment, pleasant or otherwise. 'nea' (aha) means that understanding has arrived; 'wao' only says that expectation has been exceeded.
articulatory_notes: Round the lips and raise the back of the tongue for voiced 'w', then release the rounding as the jaw opens into central 'a'. Round the lips again and draw the tongue back for mid-back 'o' in the separate second syllable. Stress 'wa'.
sound_symbolism: The mouth moves from rounded 'w' into fully open 'a' before rounding again for 'o', a conspicuous opening that offers an embodied mnemonic for surprise.
usage_notes: Pitch and vowel length can show the size or character of the surprise. Context supplies whether it is welcome or alarming.
